filter_reason: A novel multi-agent orchestration architecture for deep research/web search, addressing a real limitation of single ReAct agents - squarely in the orchestration lane.
summary: WebSwarm proposes recursive multi-agent orchestration for web search tasks that need both depth and breadth. A single ReAct-style agent is bottlenecked by one long trajectory and a finite context window, so complex research tasks force a tradeoff between going deep and covering ground; WebSwarm instead spawns and coordinates agents recursively to handle both at once. With deep-research products becoming a standard offering, architectures for decomposing and parallelizing search agents are directly relevant to this stream.
title: WebSwarm: Recursive Multi-Agent Orchestration for Deep-and-Wide Web Search
excerpt: Large language model (LLM)-based web search agents are transforming information seeking from simple factoid question answering into complex, deep-and-wide search and research-oriented tasks. A single ReAct-style agent is constrained by one long trajectory and limited context, making it difficult to handle depth and coverage simultaneously. Existing multi-agent systems improve search coverage through parallel execution and aggregation, but still exhibit clear limitations in recursive depth, collaboration adaptability, and evidence-grounded expansion.
